name: Ponce City Market food hall
description: Historic Sears building turned food hall. Grab something at the Central Food Hall and walk straight out onto the Beltline.

name: Beltline Eastside Trail
description: The classic stroll. Street art, skaters, and golden-hour people watching between Ponce City Market and Krog Street.

name: Piedmont Park
description: Atlanta's living room. Skyline views from the Active Oval, picnics by Lake Clara Meer.

name: Jasmine wall in Midtown
description: A whole wall of confederate jasmine that perfumes the block. Best at dusk when the air is warm.

name: Krog Street Tunnel
description: Ever-changing graffiti tunnel connecting Cabbagetown and Inman Park. New art every week.

name: Shoot the Hooch (Powers Island)
description: Float the Chattahoochee on a tube or kayak. Rentals at Powers Island — bring a dry bag and sunscreen.

name: Grant Park Farmers Market
description: Sunday-morning market at The Beacon. Peaches in summer, good coffee year-round.

name: Freedom Park
description: Love this park! It's empty and feels like you aren't even in a city, perfect spot for a picnic, relaxing from the crowds

name: Pollo Primo
description: This is hands-down the best roasted chicken in the city, maybe the entire US. You have to go at happy hour and get the meal

name: Bon Ton
description: Best catfish Po Boy outside of New Orleans. They are a late night spot with a clubby date night feeling

name: Landmark Theatre
description: And old timey cinema, not the best chairs our sound system but the vibes are great, good for Wes Anderson type movies

name: Apres Diem
description: Good datey spot for post-movies, they also have a nice tea sampler. Feels sort of European vibes

name: Trader Joes
description: Life is excellent when you have a Trader Joes nearby. Perfect for running down to grab a couple things to eat dinner, live life simply

name: Rumi's Kitchen
description: I have never had persian food until I moved to Atlanta, and WOW, it's good, get a lot of sabzi platters, they are free, and you can keep the meal pretty inexpensive. The eggplants are great, and so are the rices

name: Delbar
description: Another Persian gem of the city, the food is incredible, always always go for the Kashk Bademjoon and then get a rice or something like that, never fails, though it's ALWAYS busy, best is to go when it's raining or a bad day because it'll be more empty.

name: Critical Mass
description: A beautiful ride in the city, the last Friday of the month, always leaving at 6:45p SHARP. There's no better way to see the city than riding with 100s of cyclists on the road. You'll feel safe if you are a moderate rider, maybe go with a friend if it's your first time

name: Antico's Pizza
description: Greeeaaaattt Pizza in the city, it's a bit on the pricier side, but if you want good pizza, this is the place to go

name: The Goat Farm
description: This is one of the first places I ever saw when I came to Atlanta and I fell in love with it, a old decrepit steel industrial zone, resembled the feel from the movie the Time Machine when they are moving through ages. Now it's an upbeat art district with a lot of exhibitions and studios for local artists, they always have events, keep an eye for whatever is coming up.

name: Bomb Biscuit
description:
A new find, and quickly skyrocketed to a frequent stop on a Saturday/Sunday morning, the chicken biscuits are... oh.. heavenly, the kind of food that you get a crave and you can't stop until you've had it. A MUST GET. 

Go early before brunch time.

name: Chomp and Stomp
description:
Free Chili! The entire neighborhood starts on a battle for the best chili. You pay $10 to get a spoon from the local market and you are game to try all the chili.

Always the 1st Saturday of November — chili service starts 12:30pm, live music on multiple stages all day.

name: Inman Park Festival & Tour of Homes
description: Free neighborhood festival — live music, a tour of historic homes, and the eclectic Inman Park Festival Parade (starts Edgewood & Euclid, Saturday 2pm). Look out for the Krewe of the Grateful Gluttons' Gnome March tradition — pointy red hats mandatory. Runs Friday through Sunday.

name: Candler Park Fall Fest
description: Free weekend street festival in Candler Park — live music, food, and a beer garden.

name: Virginia-Highland Porchfest
description: Front porches become stages — around 100 local bands play across the neighborhood in one afternoon. Ticketed as of 2026 ($10–20).

name: Little Five Points Halloween Festival & Parade
description: Atlanta's weirdest Halloween party — costumes mandatory, everyone welcome. Parade steps off Sunday at 1pm.

name: Atlanta BeltLine Lantern Parade
description: Chantelle Rytter and the Krewe of the Grateful Gluttons lead Atlanta's biggest night parade — giant handmade lanterns along the Westside Trail (Adair Park → Lee + White). Moved back to fall for 2026. Exact times TBD — check beltline.org closer to the date.

name: Sandy Springs Lantern Parade
description: Glowing lanterns, larger-than-life puppets, live music, and food trucks through City Springs. Pre-party from 6pm, parade lines up at 8, steps off 8:30pm.

name: Butterfly Lantern Parade (Hapeville)
description: Mile-long lantern parade through downtown Hapeville, led by the Krewe of Grateful Gluttons — festival at 5pm, parade around 8:10pm. Held in March; 2026 date not yet announced (2025 edition was March 22) — check the City of Hapeville events page.

name: Parliament of Owls Lantern Parade
description: A 1.2-mile lantern-parade loop through Midtown starting and ending at Colony Square, with owl-themed puppets by the Krewe of the Grateful Gluttons. Held in August; exact 2026 date varies year to year — check colonysquare.com.

name: Where the Weird Things Are
description: Chantelle Rytter's "upside-down" parade at the Historic Fourth Ward Park Amphitheater — instead of marching, 40+ life-size puppets stay put and you stroll among them, with live music and shadow puppetry. Usually October; exact 2026 date not yet confirmed — check beltline.org.
